content: Alex is a final-year Data Science student at ENSAI Rennes.
Alex has a research-oriented profile in AI, data science, and applied machine learning.
Alex is interested in building practical AI systems that connect modeling, software engineering, and real-world deployment.
Alex is enrolled in the Data Science and Engineering program at ENSAI Rennes from 2024 to 2026.
Alex completed an Engineering Degree in Statistics and Economics at ENSAE Dakar from 2020 to 2024.
Alex worked as a Data Scientist Intern at the United Nations World Food Programme in Rome in 2025.
At the World Food Programme, Alex developed a biometric duplicate detection system for humanitarian beneficiary management.
At the World Food Programme, Alex built a biometric matching pipeline combining deep learning feature extraction and minutiae-based techniques.
At the World Food Programme, Alex worked with TensorFlow-based biometric representations and evaluation workflows on operational data.
At the World Food Programme, Alex conducted threshold optimization and performance evaluation on biometric matching systems.
At the World Food Programme, Alex contributed to reducing duplicate biometric records and reducing dependence on proprietary third-party solutions.
Alex worked as a Data Analyst Intern at the National Agency of Statistics and Demography in Dakar in 2022.
At ANSD, Alex studied the sociodemographic determinants of youth migration using logistic regression and exploratory data analysis.
At ANSD, Alex worked with census or survey data and contributed to data quality assurance workflows.
Alex has experience supporting students on statistical analysis and research-related academic work.
Alex has strong programming experience in Python.
Alex also works with SQL and R.
Alex has experience with PyTorch, TensorFlow, and scikit-learn.
Alex has used FastAPI to build REST APIs.
Alex has experience with machine learning methods including logistic regression, linear regression, SVM, bagging, boosting, clustering, and PCA.
Alex has NLP experience in text preprocessing, tokenization, TF-IDF vectorization, topic modeling, and text classification.
Alex has worked on LLM-related topics including transformers, GPT-2 fine-tuning, knowledge distillation, and self-supervised learning.
Alex has experience with information retrieval concepts such as indexing, inverted indexes, query-based retrieval, and ranking.
Alex has cloud and infrastructure experience with AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, and CI/CD tools.
Alex has used OpenCV and computer vision techniques in image-related projects.
Alex has experience with data collection, dashboards, and reporting tools such as Power BI, KoboToolBox, ODK, and Shiny.
Alex fine-tuned GPT-2 for supervised text classification and compared it with classical baselines.
Alex built a document retrieval and web indexing project with ingestion, preprocessing, indexing, and relevance ranking.
Alex developed Postagram, a serverless REST API project using AWS Lambda, S3, DynamoDB, API Gateway, FastAPI, and Terraform.
In Postagram, Alex implemented secure image upload with presigned URLs and automated image labeling with AWS Rekognition.
Alex worked on an LLM Planner Assistant that converts natural language scheduling instructions into structured JSON for calendar integration.
Alex completed a tweet classification project using an end-to-end NLP pipeline with TF-IDF, logistic regression, and random forest.
Alex completed a customer segmentation project using PCA and K-Means.
Alex worked on computer vision projects involving face detection and gender classification.
Alex has exposure to data engineering projects involving cloud ingestion pipelines, transformation layers, and analytical data modeling.
Alex is particularly interested in machine learning, deep learning, and applied AI.
Alex has recurring interest and experience in NLP, large language models, and retrieval systems.
Alex has recurring project exposure to computer vision, biometrics, and image analysis.
Alex has recurring project exposure to cloud infrastructure, APIs, and production-oriented data or AI systems.
Alex prefers concise, professional, and high-quality application emails.
Alex is especially motivated by applied AI systems that connect research, engineering, and product impact.
Alex tends to value internships where AI systems are deployed on real use cases rather than purely theoretical work.
Alex is seeking a final-year internship starting around March or April 2026.
Alex targets internships in Data Science, AI, NLP, LLM systems, automation, and agentic AI.
Alex is interested in roles involving retrieval pipelines, RAG architectures, prompt engineering, and AI product development.
Alex is open to pursuing research-oriented opportunities and potentially a PhD after industry experience.
Alex works in French and English.
Alex also lists Spanish among his working languages.
